rows.description: Computus Francisci Webber Rector in a 2d die November 1766 ad eundem diem revolution 1769.
rows.notes: Top-of-page title is partly faded and written in a long running hand. Year figures (1766, 1769) and the name 'Francis(ci) Webber' are legible but several words (e.g. the Latin phrasing) are abbreviated and uncertain—transcribed to best reading.
rows.section_header: Computus Francisci Webber Rector in a 2d die November 1766 ad eundem diem revolution 1769.

rows.notes: Centered section header. The script is fairly clear; spelling is archaic/abbreviated but the intended heading 'Arrearagia Recepta' (Arrearage Receipts) is readable.
Clear alignment with ruling lines. Pounds '3', shillings '12' and pence '8' are unambiguous and within valid ranges. Description 'Morton ad feet annum' legible.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from the Morton estate.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from the Morton estate, amounting to £3 12s 8d.

rows.notes: Amounts align clearly: £3 4s 8d. Description is a short dash + 'ad feet Mill' (mill fees); wording slightly abbreviated but unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Annual mill rent from Morton (same as previous Morton entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual mill rent from Morton, amounting to £3 4s 8d.

rows.notes: Numbers are very clear: £15 0s 0d. Description 'Yardington Rect pro ann: & dim:' reads clearly as rector's receipt for year and half-year.
rows.english_description: Receipts from the Yardington rectory covering one year and a half.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts from the Yardington rectory for one year and a half, amounting to £15 0s 0d.

rows.description: Jenner: pro grain & 3 by part. an:
rows.notes: Amount reads £32 14s 2½d. The halfpenny symbol (a small loop/ob) is clear; alignment with column lines is consistent. Description contains abbreviations (hand written), interpreted as shown.
rows.english_description: Payment from Jenner for grain and associated annual charges for the year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Jenner for grain and associated annual charges, amounting to £32 14s 2½d.

rows.notes: Amount reads £15 13s 5½d. Halfpenny symbol visible. 'Kidlington Mills' reading is clear in context of place names.
rows.english_description: Income from the Kidlington mills.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Income from the Kidlington mills, amounting to £15 13s 5½d.

rows.notes: Amount reads £5 11s 7¾d: pence shows three-farthings symbol ('3q' or '3/4') which is legible. The place/name is somewhat abbreviated; chosen reading 'Garrington Fodler' based on letter shapes but not certain.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Garrington for fodder or farm produce.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Garrington for fodder, amounting to £5 11s 7¾d.

rows.notes: Second identical-looking line: £5 11s 7¾d. Fraction three-farthings clearly present. 'Molineux ditto' (ditto to prior) inferred from the script; name reading reasonably clear.
rows.english_description: Annual fodder payment from Molineux (same category and period as Garrington).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual fodder payment from Molineux, amounting to £5 11s 7¾d.

rows.notes: Amount reads £1 14s 0d; digits align well. Description 'Southnewington' is legible; 'Canon' / 'Canon' reading fits the script though could be 'Canon' or similar office name.
rows.english_description: Annual canonical/clerical receipt from South Newington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual canonical receipt from South Newington, amounting to £1 14s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads £4 7s 0½d — the pence shows a halfpenny mark (small loop) after the zero. Place name appears as 'Bensington' (legible); small uncertainty in final letter but context supports this reading.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from Bensington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from Bensington, amounting to £4 7s ½d.

rows.notes: Amount £9 6s 8d is clear. The place/name is less clear in the handwriting ('Ledene', 'Ledam', or similar); transcribed conservatively as 'Ledene Compy'.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from the Ledene company (rents or dues).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from the Ledene company, amounting to £9 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount £6 13s 4d is legible and within valid ranges. The name looks like 'Harpforth' or 'Heaforth' in the script; chosen 'Harpforth' as best fit though some letters are ambiguous.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Harpforth (same kind of receipt as the Ledene company).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Harpforth, amounting to £6 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Amount £1 13s 4d clear. Description likely 'Greenhow pro ditto' (pro ditto = as above); some contraction in the last word but name 'Greenhow' readable.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Greenhow (same as the preceding Ledene company receipts).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Greenhow, amounting to £1 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Subtotal line clearly written as £105 2s 8d. Matches the sum of the preceding itemised amounts (validation done). 'E:' marking visible in the margin.
Amount £68 0s 0d clearly written. Description appears to duplicate earlier place name with 'Rect pro an & dim' (rector receipts); wording is slightly abbreviated but legible.
rows.english_description: Receipts from the South Newington rectory covering one year and a half.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts from the South Newington rectory for one year and a half, amounting to £68 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount £60 0s 0d is clear. Description reads as a rectory receipt for Kidlington (the last word interpreted as 'Grain' or 'Grin' in the script); reading of final word slightly uncertain but context supports 'Rect'.
rows.english_description: Receipts from the Kidlington rectory in respect of grain.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from the Kidlington rectory for grain, amounting to £60 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads £0 10s 0d (10 shillings). The place/name is written compactly; transcribed as 'Lidenhall' which matches letter shapes in the entry.
rows.english_description: Annual payment received from Lidenhall.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment received from Lidenhall, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount £6 0s 0d clear. Place name is uncertain (reads like 'Gotherwan' or 'Gotherwan'); chosen transcription reflects visible letter forms but is not certain.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from Gotherwan.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from Gotherwan, amounting to £6 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Subtotal line under the block with amounts £68, £60, 10s, and £6; the total £154 10s 0d is clearly written and aligns with the column rules.
Centered new-section header; handwriting is somewhat stylised. Read as 'Recepta anni Presentis' (Receipts of the present year); a few letters abbreviated so moderate confidence.

Amount £90 5s 0d is clear. The description is less clear: the first word 'Kidlington' is readable, 'augment' appears as an abbreviation, and the final word is uncertain (read here as 'Castlemens' as closest fit to the handwriting). Lower confidence due to uncertain final word.
rows.english_description: Augmentation receipt for Kidlington associated with Castlemens (top-up grant to the living).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation receipt for Kidlington associated with Castlemens, amounting to £90 5s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount £2 15s 0d clear. Description begins with a dash and two surnames/parties; letters ambiguous (could be 'Copes & Brown' or 'Copps & Brewers'). Transcribed as 'Copps & Brewers' with note of ambiguity.
rows.english_description: Additional augmentation receipt for Kidlington from Copps and Brewers (same augmentation group as previous).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Additional augmentation receipt for Kidlington from Copps and Brewers, amounting to £2 15s 0d.

rows.description: South Newington Rect: pro dim. an: ad Sept. annum:
rows.notes: Amount £28 0s 0d is clear. Description appears to be for South Newington rector pro dim (half-year) with a note 'ad Sept. annum' (to September year). Some abbreviation and punctuation cause moderate uncertainty but overall readable.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly receipt from the South Newington rectory up to Michaelmas/September.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly receipt from the South Newington rectory to September, amounting to £28 0s 0d.

rows.description: Yardington Rect: pro dim. an: ad Sept. annum:
rows.notes: Amount £5 0s 0d clear. Description parallels the previous line for Yardington; abbreviations (pro dim, ad Sept) are readable in context.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly receipt from the Yardington rectory to September.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly receipt from the Yardington rectory to September, amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 10s 0d is clear. The place/name looks like 'Lidenhall' or 'Lidenhall' but letters are small and compressed; transcribed conservatively with moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Small receipt from Lidenhall (specific nature not stated).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Small receipt from Lidenhall, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Final total line is clearly written as £126 10s 0d beneath the last group; aligns with column rulings and is unambiguous.
